g.text: Which of the following best describes the fundamental purpose of utilizing dimensional analysis in physical calculations?
To calculate the exact numerical value of physical constants.
To verify the consistency of equations by checking if both sides share the same dimensions.
To convert between different measurement systems without needing conversion factors.
To eliminate the need for significant figures in final answers.
enter a, b, c, or d
Dimensional analysis is used to ensure that an equation is physically consistent by verifying that the dimensions on both sides of the equals sign match.
When measuring an object, what is the primary difference between accuracy and precision?
Accuracy refers to how close measurements are to each other; precision refers to how close they are to the true value.
Accuracy and precision are interchangeable terms for the same concept.
Accuracy refers to how close a measurement is to the true value; precision refers to the consistency or repeatability of the measurement.
Precision is only relevant to digital instruments, while accuracy is only for analog instruments.
enter a, b, c, or d
Accuracy measures proximity to a true value, while precision measures the reliability or repeatability of the measurement process.
How many significant figures are present in the measurement 0.0045060 meters?
